summary: Decomposed the bizPA MVP PRD into granular backend, UI, governance, sync, export, and QA tasks aligned to the MVP build priority and quarter-ready export outcome.
warnings: The PRD is feature-rich but under-specifies technical architecture, so task outputs assume a local-first app with API, persistence, background workers, and a web or desktop-viewable UI surface.
Biometric confirmation is treated as an integration boundary and policy hook in MVP unless a concrete platform biometric provider already exists.
UI tasks include viewability requirements inferred from the UI delivery skill: starter scripts, localhost access details, smoke startup validation, and screenshot evidence.
Accounting connectors are scoped as export/mapping contracts rather than live third-party integrations because direct integrations are explicitly de-prioritized for MVP.

tasks.title: Define canonical business entity and quarter schemas
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the flat, export-friendly, immutable data structures that every bizPA workflow depends on.
tasks.purpose: Create the canonical schema set for monetary entities, non-monetary entities, quarter references, counterparties, and snapshots so downstream capture, readiness, export, and sync logic all use the same contract.
tasks.input: PRD sections 9-10, 15-18, and 26-29 defining entity types, required fields, quarter behavior, and MVP scope.
tasks.output: Versioned schema definitions for core entities, quarter state, and snapshot metadata, plus a short schema decision record covering included and excluded bookkeeping concepts.
tasks.action: Design and document normalized schema contracts for invoices, receipts, payments, quotes, bookings, notes, attachments, reminders, clients, suppliers, quarters, and snapshots with explicit required/optional fields and invariant rules.
verification: Schema definitions cover all MVP monetary and non-monetary entity types from the PRD.
Required and optional fields are documented with data types, constraints, and example payloads.
Excluded bookkeeping concepts such as journal entries and chart of accounts are explicitly omitted from the schema contract.

tasks.title: Design immutable event ledger schema and append-only history rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the flat, export-friendly, immutable data structures that every bizPA workflow depends on.
tasks.purpose: Provide the append-only event model that underpins the Inbox, auditability, readiness explainability, snapshot diffs, and governance history.
tasks.input: A1 schema contracts and PRD sections 11, 13, 15, 16, 21, 22, and 25 covering immutable history and auditability.
tasks.output: Event ledger schema, event taxonomy, append rules, and a mapping between entity actions and emitted business events.
tasks.action: Define an append-only event contract for business events, governance events, snapshot events, and correction events, including ordering rules, immutable payload snapshots, and linkage to source entities.
verification: Every mutable business action in MVP maps to a new event rather than in-place history edits.
Event types cover inbox-visible business events and governance actions without mixing in technical/system noise.
The schema supports reconstructing quarter history and explaining readiness or snapshot changes.

tasks.title: Implement VAT calculation and monetary validation utilities
tasks.workstream_goal: Establish the flat, export-friendly, immutable data structures that every bizPA workflow depends on.
tasks.purpose: Centralize deterministic monetary calculations and validation so previews, commits, readiness scoring, exports, and snapshots all derive the same totals and rule outcomes.
tasks.input: A1 entity schemas, PRD sections 9, 10, 13, 14, and 17 covering VAT, net/gross consistency, and trust requirements.
tasks.output: Reusable monetary calculation module with validation rules for VAT rate handling, amount consistency, and invalid commit prevention.
tasks.action: Build shared utilities that compute and validate VAT and totals, flag missing or inconsistent monetary fields, and expose deterministic error codes for use across the product.
verification: Given the same inputs, calculation outputs are deterministic across capture, readiness, snapshot, and export contexts.
Validation rejects inconsistent net/VAT/gross combinations and missing mandatory VAT metadata for committed monetary items.
Automated tests cover zero VAT, standard VAT, missing VAT rate, and malformed monetary payload cases.

tasks.title: Define voice and manual capture intent contract
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn voice, manual, and attachment capture into structured records with low friction and clear commit behavior.
tasks.purpose: Specify how freeform voice and manual inputs map onto bizPA entity types, parsed fields, confidence scores, and preview requirements.
tasks.input: PRD sections 9, 24, and 27 describing supported inputs, examples, and confirmation behavior.
tasks.output: Capture intent specification covering supported utterances, form payloads, parsed field mapping, confidence semantics, and unsupported fallback handling.
tasks.action: Document and implement the parser contract for invoices, receipts, quotes, payments, bookings, notes, and reminders, including confidence thresholds and ambiguity handling.
verification: All MVP capture examples in the PRD map to a concrete supported intent and preview payload.
The contract distinguishes monetary preview-required flows from non-monetary auto-commit flows.
Low-confidence parsing paths produce explicit review flags rather than silent assumptions.

tasks.title: Build monetary composition preview and commit pipeline
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn voice, manual, and attachment capture into structured records with low friction and clear commit behavior.
tasks.purpose: Implement the core Capture -> Parse -> Preview -> Confirm -> Commit flow for monetary items so trusted records only exist after explicit or policy-allowed commit.
tasks.input: A1-A3 schemas and rules, B1 capture contract, and PRD section 9 monetary flow requirements.
tasks.output: Composition-state service and commit pipeline that persists confirmed monetary entities, emits events, recalculates readiness, and triggers sync.
tasks.action: Create the composition model, preview serializer, confirm/edit/cancel actions, and final commit transaction that writes the entity and event atomically.
verification: Unconfirmed monetary compositions do not appear in the Inbox, readiness metrics, or final sync payloads.
Confirming a preview creates the entity and corresponding immutable event in one transaction.
Edit and cancel paths leave no committed financial truth behind.

tasks.title: Implement non-monetary quick capture and attachment intake
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn voice, manual, and attachment capture into structured records with low friction and clear commit behavior.
tasks.purpose: Enable low-friction note, reminder, attachment, non-valued booking, and lightweight reference capture that commits immediately without polluting monetary workflows.
tasks.input: A1 entity schemas, B1 capture contract, and PRD section 9 non-monetary capture rules.
tasks.output: Non-monetary capture handlers, attachment metadata flow, and event emission for instant-commit items.
tasks.action: Implement direct commit handlers for non-monetary inputs, including attachment association and event creation, while keeping these records separate from monetary validation rules.
verification: Non-monetary captures commit immediately and appear in business history where relevant.
Attachments are stored as linked metadata rather than forcing monetary commit behavior.
Readiness and VAT calculations are unaffected by non-monetary commits unless explicitly linked by later rules.

tasks.title: Add capture confidence and forced-review safeguards
tasks.workstream_goal: Turn voice, manual, and attachment capture into structured records with low friction and clear commit behavior.
tasks.purpose: Prevent unsafe automatic commits or misleading previews when parsing confidence is low or sensitive conditions are detected.
tasks.input: B1 capture confidence rules, B2 commit pipeline, and PRD sections 9 and 21 safety override guidance.
tasks.output: Confidence scoring thresholds, review flags, and forced-confirmation rules integrated into capture handling.
tasks.action: Implement policy-driven logic that forces manual review for low-confidence or high-risk monetary captures and marks affected records for follow-up.
verification: Low-confidence monetary items cannot bypass required confirmation paths.
Forced-review reasons are surfaced as structured error or warning codes for UI and readiness use.
Automated tests cover threshold breaches, ambiguous counterparties, and sensitive action types.

tasks.title: Build inbox timeline query model and filter API
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose business history and entity context in a way that is chronological, explainable, and safe to act on.
tasks.purpose: Power the business activity Inbox with strict chronological ordering, business-only events, filter support, and badge-ready payloads.
tasks.input: A2 event ledger contract and PRD section 11 Inbox requirements.
tasks.output: Inbox query service or API returning chronological event items, filter results, badge metadata, and atomic action affordances.
tasks.action: Implement the read model that translates immutable events into inbox cards, applies supported filters, and exposes safe atomic actions without allowing event edits.
verification: Inbox results are newest-first and exclude system noise such as sync internals or API logs.
Supported filters return consistent subsets for Needs Review, Financial, Quotes, Payments, and Alerts.
Atomic action payloads resolve back to entity operations without permitting direct event mutation.

tasks.title: Deliver business activity inbox screen with filters and atomic actions
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose business history and entity context in a way that is chronological, explainable, and safe to act on.
tasks.purpose: Provide the user-facing Inbox experience that makes business events scannable, filterable, and actionable while preserving immutable history semantics.
tasks.input: C1 inbox API, A2 event taxonomy, and PRD section 11 display and action requirements.
tasks.output: A viewable Inbox screen with filters, badges, event cards, and atomic action entry points for supported entity updates.
tasks.action: Implement the Inbox UI and connect it to timeline data, showing event metadata, filter controls, badge states, and safe action affordances for mark paid, due-date update, category fix, counterparty link, and quote conversion.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the UI stack and prints the Inbox access URL.
The Inbox is reachable at a documented localhost URL and loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run.
Representative Inbox data renders with filters, badges, and atomic action controls working for at least one financial and one alert event.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
Attempting to edit or delete an event directly is blocked by the UI flow.

tasks.title: Build deep-dive entity views for invoices receipts quotes and payments
tasks.workstream_goal: Expose business history and entity context in a way that is chronological, explainable, and safe to act on.
tasks.purpose: Let users inspect a single business item in full detail, including totals, status, attachments, and entity-specific history, without mixing unrelated records.
tasks.input: A1 entity schemas, A2 event ledger, and PRD section 12 deep-dive requirements.
tasks.output: Entity detail screens and supporting data endpoints for invoice, receipt, quote, and payment records.
tasks.action: Implement deep-dive views and data loaders that show entity-only context, correction state, and allowed next actions based on lifecycle status.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the UI stack and prints the deep-dive access URL or base localhost URL.
The entity detail screens load without startup crashes and render representative invoice and receipt records in a smoke run.
Screenshot evidence is captured for at least two entity types to the epic verification folder using task id naming.
Correction-state actions shown in deep dive follow allowed lifecycle transitions and do not expose in-place amount edits.
The view excludes unrelated client history or unrelated business records.

tasks.title: Enforce monetary immutability and correction state machine
tasks.workstream_goal: Protect financial truth after commit and constrain quarter behavior through explicit lifecycle transitions.
tasks.purpose: Prevent deletion and in-place mutation of committed monetary truth while enabling controlled correction flows such as void, replace, refund, and supersede.
tasks.input: A1-A3 schema and validation work plus PRD sections 13 and 16 lifecycle rules.
tasks.output: Domain rules and transition engine for quote, invoice, receipt, payment, and quarter lifecycle states, including correction events.
tasks.action: Implement state transition validation that blocks forbidden post-commit amount edits and routes corrections through explicit replacement or void flows with event logging.
verification: Committed monetary entities cannot be deleted or have amounts edited in place through any service path.
Allowed transitions for Quote, Invoice, and Quarter states are validated against a single authoritative state machine.
Correction flows emit new events and preserve traceability between original and replacement records.

tasks.title: Implement quarter close and reopen governance flow
tasks.workstream_goal: Protect financial truth after commit and constrain quarter behavior through explicit lifecycle transitions.
tasks.purpose: Support optional manual quarter close with explicit reopen controls, reason capture, and audit logging so closed periods are protected without blocking necessary corrections forever.
tasks.input: A1 quarter schema, A2 event ledger, D1 state machine, and PRD section 16 close/reopen rules.
tasks.output: Quarter lifecycle service enforcing active, snapshot, closed, and reopened behaviors with audit records and policy hooks for biometric confirmation.
tasks.action: Build the close and reopen flow that blocks new monetary entries for closed quarters, requires explicit reopen confirmation and reason, and reactivates diff and readiness logic when reopened.
verification: Closing a quarter blocks new monetary entries and quarter-impacting edits through validated service checks.
Reopening requires a structured reason and persists an immutable governance event for the action.
Post-reopen behavior re-enables readiness recalculation and snapshot diff evaluation for the affected quarter.

tasks.title: Define readiness issue taxonomy and deterministic scoring weights
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate and explain how ready the active quarter is for export and what still needs fixing.
tasks.purpose: Translate the PRD readiness philosophy into a concrete scoring and issue model that is explainable, weighted, and limited to the active quarter.
tasks.input: PRD section 14 readiness requirements and sections 25-29 on trust, explainability, and MVP priorities.
tasks.output: Readiness rules specification covering issue types, severities, weights, score formulas, and no-retroactive-change rules for snapshots.
tasks.action: Design a deterministic readiness scoring model with tiered issue categories, active-quarter scope, and explicit explanation payloads for UI and export integrity reports.
verification: The taxonomy covers Tier 1 critical integrity issues, Tier 2 structural completeness issues, and Tier 3 optional quality signals.
The scoring model is deterministic and explainable for the same quarter state.
The specification explicitly excludes live score recalculation for past snapshots.

tasks.title: Implement active-quarter readiness calculation and explainability API
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate and explain how ready the active quarter is for export and what still needs fixing.
tasks.purpose: Produce live readiness scores, issue counts, severity breakdowns, and actionable drill-down data for the active quarter only.
tasks.input: E1 readiness specification, A1 schemas, A2 events, and D1 lifecycle rules.
tasks.output: Readiness engine service with calculation jobs or queries plus an explainability endpoint returning issue lists and fix links.
tasks.action: Implement the calculation layer that evaluates current-quarter entities and events against readiness rules, returns scores and issue detail, and updates immediately on relevant business changes.
verification: The engine recalculates only for the active quarter and leaves stored snapshot readiness values unchanged.
Issue output includes enough context to explain the missing percentage and link users back to the affected records.
Automated tests cover duplicate items, missing category, missing counterparty, missing VAT rate, and post-snapshot change scenarios.

tasks.title: Deliver readiness dashboard and issue drill-down workflow
tasks.workstream_goal: Continuously calculate and explain how ready the active quarter is for export and what still needs fixing.
tasks.purpose: Expose readiness as a professional operational metric with actionable drill-down so users can understand and fix quarter blockers quickly.
tasks.input: E2 readiness API, C3 deep-dive navigation, and PRD section 14 output and drill-down requirements.
tasks.output: A viewable readiness screen or panel showing score, issue counts, severity, and links into fix flows.
tasks.action: Build the readiness UI that shows the current quarter score, issue inventory, weighted severity, and navigation into record-level remediation flows.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the readiness UI and prints the localhost access URL.
The readiness screen loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run and displays representative score and issue data.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows score, issue count, and drill-down rows with links to relevant fix flows.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
The UI avoids gamified patterns and uses explainable labels for score changes and issue weights.

tasks.title: Implement immutable snapshot creation and persistence
tasks.workstream_goal: Create frozen quarter snapshots that can always be generated, re-downloaded, and versioned when live quarter data changes.
tasks.purpose: Turn the active quarter state into a stored immutable snapshot package record without hard-blocking export when unresolved issues remain.
tasks.input: A1 snapshot schema, A2 event ledger, E2 readiness outputs, and PRD section 15 snapshot rules.
tasks.output: Snapshot creation service that stores snapshot metadata, included transaction ids, totals, readiness-at-export, warning summary, and generated file references.
tasks.action: Build the snapshot transaction that materializes quarter data into a frozen snapshot record, associates integrity warnings, and preserves export-ready metadata for later download.
verification: At least one snapshot can be generated for an active quarter even when unresolved issues exist.
Created snapshots do not recalculate or change when live quarter data changes later.
Snapshot records include transaction membership, totals, readiness-at-export, and generated file references.

tasks.title: Build changed-since-last-snapshot detection and version diff engine
tasks.workstream_goal: Create frozen quarter snapshots that can always be generated, re-downloaded, and versioned when live quarter data changes.
tasks.purpose: Detect when a quarter has materially changed after a snapshot, compute diffs for later versions, and block redundant version creation when nothing changed.
tasks.input: F1 snapshot persistence, A2 event ledger, D1 correction states, and PRD section 15 diff/version rules.
tasks.output: Diff engine and quarter-change flagging logic that produces added, voided, adjusted, revenue impact, and VAT impact summaries.
tasks.action: Implement change detection between the latest snapshot and the live quarter state, including user-facing diff summaries and no-change prevention logic for redundant versions.
verification: Post-snapshot quarter changes set a changed-since-last-snapshot flag without mutating the previous snapshot.
Generating a new version with no quarter changes is prevented with a clear no-change result.
Diff outputs include transaction additions, voids or corrections, and revenue/VAT impact summaries.

tasks.title: Deliver snapshot management and version comparison UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Create frozen quarter snapshots that can always be generated, re-downloaded, and versioned when live quarter data changes.
tasks.purpose: Give users a clear UI to create snapshots, inspect integrity warnings, compare changes since the last version, and re-download older snapshot packages.
tasks.input: F1 snapshot service, F2 diff engine, G2 export service, and PRD sections 15 and 17.
tasks.output: Viewable snapshot management screens for create, inspect, compare, and re-download actions.
tasks.action: Build the snapshot UI flows that let users generate a first snapshot, inspect warnings, review version diffs, and re-download prior export packages without overwriting history.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the snapshot UI and prints the localhost access URL.
The snapshot management screen loads without startup crashes in a smoke run and supports creating or previewing a snapshot flow.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows snapshot versions, warning summaries, and compare or re-download controls.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
The UI blocks redundant version generation when no changes exist and shows the expected no-change message.

tasks.title: Define accountant-ready export package contract
tasks.workstream_goal: Generate one-click export packages that accountants can use without spreadsheet cleanup.
tasks.purpose: Specify the exact package structure, file naming, CSV fields, summary document contents, and integrity report contents required for trustworthy handoff.
tasks.input: A1 schema contracts, F1 snapshot metadata, and PRD section 17 export requirements.
tasks.output: Export package specification describing CSV schema, VAT summary document, integrity report, metadata manifest, and naming conventions.
tasks.action: Create a precise export contract that defines accountant-ready files, ordering, metadata, and packaging rules for initial export and later re-downloads.
verification: The package definition includes structured CSV, VAT summary document or equivalent, integrity report, snapshot metadata, version number, and quarter label.
CSV field ordering and formatting are defined to minimize accountant cleanup or reformatting.
Historic snapshots can be packaged consistently using the same contract.

tasks.title: Implement export package generation and historic re-download service
tasks.workstream_goal: Generate one-click export packages that accountants can use without spreadsheet cleanup.
tasks.purpose: Produce downloadable export bundles for current and historic snapshots using the approved package contract and immutable snapshot data.
tasks.input: G1 export contract, F1 snapshot records, F2 diff/change signals, and E2 readiness explanation data.
tasks.output: Export generation service that creates package files for snapshots and exposes deterministic re-download behavior for historic versions.
tasks.action: Build the file generation and retrieval flow that packages snapshot data into accountant-ready bundles and supports repeat download of prior versions without regeneration drift.
verification: Export bundles generated from the same snapshot are reproducible and do not drift over time.
Historic snapshot versions can be re-downloaded independently and return the correct versioned package.
Generated packages include all required files and surface unresolved integrity warnings rather than hiding them.

tasks.title: Define export mapping contracts for accountant and filing-adjacent connectors
tasks.workstream_goal: Generate one-click export packages that accountants can use without spreadsheet cleanup.
tasks.purpose: Prepare clean mapping boundaries for Xero-compatible, QuickBooks-compatible, and generic accountant-ready exports without overbuilding live integrations.
tasks.input: G1 export package contract and PRD section 23 integration categories and MVP constraints.
tasks.output: Connector mapping specification for export variants and transformation rules from canonical bizPA data to target formats.
tasks.action: Define transformation contracts for generic accountant export and future Xero or QuickBooks-compatible mappings while keeping MVP focused on export compatibility rather than direct API integrations.
verification: Mapping rules are derived from the canonical bizPA schema rather than ad hoc export-only fields.
At least one generic accountant-ready mapping and placeholders for Xero-compatible and QuickBooks-compatible mapping are documented.
The contract does not require direct third-party API integration for MVP completion.

tasks.title: Design local-first sync envelope and offline queue model
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide resilient backup and continuity sync without compromising immutable financial truth.
tasks.purpose: Define the MVP sync architecture that treats local storage as primary, sync as background, and immutable financial rules as the guardrail against silent corruption.
tasks.input: A1 schemas, A2 event ledger, D1 integrity rules, and PRD section 18 sync requirements.
tasks.output: Sync architecture contract for delta envelopes, offline queue entries, retry semantics, tenant isolation, and conflict policy boundaries.
tasks.action: Design the sync payload and queue model for local-first persistence, encrypted transport, background sync, offline replay, and financially safe last-write-wins behavior where allowed.
verification: The design distinguishes local committed truth from background cloud synchronization state.
Financial immutability constraints are preserved even when last-write-wins is used for acceptable non-financial cases.
Queue, retry, and tenant-isolation fields are defined clearly enough for worker implementation.

tasks.title: Implement resilient background sync worker and conflict handling
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide resilient backup and continuity sync without compromising immutable financial truth.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the actual sync runtime that uploads local changes in the background, survives offline periods, and avoids silent corruption on replay or conflict.
tasks.input: H1 sync architecture, A2 event ledger, D1 integrity rules, and PRD section 18 MVP sync model.
tasks.output: Background sync worker with offline queue replay, encrypted transport hooks, retry logic, and conflict handling outcomes.
tasks.action: Implement the worker or service that batches local deltas, retries safely, records sync outcomes, and applies conflict resolution consistent with immutable financial behavior.
verification: Offline-created changes queue locally and replay successfully when connectivity is restored.
Sync failures do not mutate or lose local committed financial data.
Conflict handling records explicit outcomes and blocks silent corruption for immutable monetary records.

tasks.title: Build homepage command centre UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Make bizPA feel useful every day by surfacing momentum, attention, and capture actions from a single command centre.
tasks.purpose: Replace a passive home screen with the business-focused command centre described in the PRD so users immediately see what matters today.
tasks.input: PRD section 20 homepage requirements, E2 readiness data, J1 notification severity model, and I2 aggregation outputs.
tasks.output: A viewable homepage with live momentum bar, attention panel, compact performance grid, smart insight feed, and central capture action.
tasks.action: Implement the homepage UI layout and interactions, preserving the business-focused tone and emphasizing attention, performance, and rapid capture entry.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the homepage UI and prints the localhost access URL.
The homepage loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run and renders representative momentum, attention, performance, and insight data.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows a central capture action and a home screen that answers what needs attention today.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
The UI avoids gamification and exposes clear operational status rather than a passive menu.

tasks.title: Implement homepage metrics and insight aggregation services
tasks.workstream_goal: Make bizPA feel useful every day by surfacing momentum, attention, and capture actions from a single command centre.
tasks.purpose: Provide the data layer behind the homepage so momentum, attention, compact metrics, and smart insights are computed consistently from business data.
tasks.input: A1 entity schemas, A2 event ledger, E2 readiness outputs, and J1 notification rules.
tasks.output: Homepage aggregation services or queries for revenue momentum, overdue items, performance tile counts, and insight feed candidates.
tasks.action: Build the service layer that derives homepage metrics and prioritizes insights from current business state and notification signals.
verification: Weekly revenue and period delta calculations are deterministic for the same data set.
Attention items prioritize actionable issues such as overdue invoices, readiness blockers, or quarter deadlines.
Aggregation outputs feed the homepage without duplicating business rules already owned by readiness or notifications.

tasks.title: Implement notification event model and prioritization rules
tasks.workstream_goal: Surface useful, actionable, non-spammy alerts that improve daily product value without diluting tax readiness.
tasks.purpose: Translate critical, important, and informational notification categories into explicit trigger rules and payload structures that can feed the homepage and notification surfaces.
tasks.input: A2 event ledger, E2 readiness changes, F2 snapshot change signals, and PRD section 19 notification philosophy.
tasks.output: Notification model and rule engine for trigger generation, severity classification, deduplication, and visibility windows.
tasks.action: Implement notification generation rules for overdue invoices, export out-of-date states, VAT deadlines, quote follow-up, client inactivity, readiness drops, snapshot creation, and invoice paid events.
verification: Notification rules cover critical, important, and informational categories from the PRD.
Generated notifications are actionable and carry a target for drill-down or remediation.
Deduplication or suppression rules reduce spammy repeats for the same underlying condition.

tasks.title: Deliver notification surfaces for alerts and insight feed entries
tasks.workstream_goal: Surface useful, actionable, non-spammy alerts that improve daily product value without diluting tax readiness.
tasks.purpose: Expose notifications to end users in a visible but controlled UI that supports daily awareness and links directly into fixes or follow-up actions.
tasks.input: J1 notification engine, I1 homepage UI, and PRD sections 19-20.
tasks.output: Notification center or embedded alert surfaces integrated into homepage and relevant screens.
tasks.action: Implement the user-facing alert and insight surfaces that show critical and important notifications prominently while allowing informational items to live in lower-noise placements.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the notification UI surface and prints the localhost access URL.
The notification surface loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run and shows representative critical, important, and informational items.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows actionable alert entries that link to the relevant business record or workflow.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
The surface remains visible and useful without overwhelming the user with repeated duplicates.

tasks.title: Define governance domain model for roles policies and audit
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide a separate administrative layer for security, configuration, service control, and audit visibility.
tasks.purpose: Create the underlying governance model for roles, feature policies, policy caps, audit logging, and service access controls needed by the control centre.
tasks.input: PRD section 22 governance scope, section 21 auto-commit policies, and section 25 safety/auditability requirements.
tasks.output: Governance schema and policy model for RBAC, audit records, feature toggles, threshold settings, VAT defaults, numbering formats, and service/plugin access.
tasks.action: Design the admin-facing domain model that represents users, roles, policy caps, feature toggles, security settings, and audit trails in a way that can drive the control centre UI and enforcement layers.
verification: The model covers owner, admin, staff, and future accountant read-only role concepts.
Policy areas include auto-commit caps, readiness rules, default VAT settings, thresholds, and feature toggles.
All governance changes are representable as auditable records with actor and timestamp.

tasks.title: Build control centre UI for governance and system health
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide a separate administrative layer for security, configuration, service control, and audit visibility.
tasks.purpose: Deliver the administrative UI where owners or admins can inspect security, policies, user access, service status, and platform health without mixing it into the end-user cockpit.
tasks.input: K1 governance model, H2 sync status, J1 notification data, and PRD section 22 control centre requirements.
tasks.output: Viewable control centre screens for access management, configuration, service/plugin status, sync health, queue backlog, and audit activity.
tasks.action: Implement the control centre UI and connect it to governance and health data so admins can manage policy caps, toggles, users, service access, and operational status.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the control centre UI and prints the localhost access URL.
The control centre loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run and shows representative governance and health data.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows policy controls, user access areas, and system health widgets for sync or queue status.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
Administrative areas are clearly separated from the end-user homepage and business Inbox surfaces.

tasks.title: Implement plugin registry service health and billing integration points
tasks.workstream_goal: Provide a separate administrative layer for security, configuration, service control, and audit visibility.
tasks.purpose: Lay the backend foundations for service enablement, plugin deployment visibility, tier gating, and future billing integrations called for in the governance scope.
tasks.input: K1 governance model and PRD sections 22-23 covering plugin deployment, payment and subscription, and service access.
tasks.output: Backend services and contracts for plugin registry entries, version tracking, staged rollout metadata, rollback markers, subscription status hooks, and service health summaries.
tasks.action: Implement service-layer contracts for plugin and service management plus billing and tier-gating integration points, without overbuilding full marketplace behavior for MVP.
verification: Plugin registry records support enable or disable state, version tracking, and rollout metadata.
Subscription and tier-gating hooks exist as integration points even if a full payment workflow is not yet implemented.
Service health summaries can be surfaced in the control centre without leaking low-level system noise into the business Inbox.

tasks.title: Implement auto-commit policy engine and safety overrides
tasks.workstream_goal: Allow experienced users to bypass some confirmations under explicit governance controls without compromising trust.
tasks.purpose: Encode the rules that determine whether auto-commit can be enabled, how long it lasts, and when confirmation must still be forced despite the mode being active.
tasks.input: K1 governance policies, B2 commit pipeline, B4 confidence safeguards, and PRD section 21 auto-commit rules.
tasks.output: Policy engine enforcing owner caps, user eligibility, duration bounds, expiry behavior, threshold overrides, and audit logging hooks for auto-commit mode.
tasks.action: Implement the server or domain-side rules for enabling, expiring, disabling, and overriding auto-commit based on tenant policy, user action, confidence, amount threshold, and sensitive action types.
verification: Auto-commit cannot be enabled unless owner policy, user opt-in, risk acknowledgement, and biometric hooks are all satisfied.
Daily, weekly, and monthly durations work within owner policy caps and never create a forever mode.
Safety overrides force manual confirmation for high amount, low confidence, or sensitive actions even while auto-commit is active.

tasks.title: Deliver auto-commit activation and visible mode UI
tasks.workstream_goal: Allow experienced users to bypass some confirmations under explicit governance controls without compromising trust.
tasks.purpose: Make auto-commit status impossible to miss and give users a controlled activation flow with clear expiry, risk acknowledgement, and undo affordances.
tasks.input: L1 policy engine, B2 capture UI flow, and PRD section 21 visibility requirements.
tasks.output: User-facing auto-commit activation flow plus persistent banners or badges, altered capture cues, and short undo affordance for eligible actions.
tasks.action: Implement the activation and visibility UI that requires acknowledgement and duration selection, shows persistent auto-commit state throughout the product, and provides a short undo window where allowed.
verification: A starter script is added or updated to launch the UI with auto-commit surfaces and prints the localhost access URL.
The auto-commit UI loads without startup dependency crashes in a smoke run and shows activation, active-state, and expired-state behavior.
Opening the documented localhost URL shows a persistent visible mode indicator and a changed capture cue while auto-commit is active.
Screenshot evidence is captured to the epic verification folder using the task id naming convention.
The UI forces confirmation when policy engine safety overrides are triggered despite active mode.

tasks.title: Create end-to-end workflow test harness for MVP critical journeys
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove that the MVP's high-risk business workflows behave predictably and safely from capture through export.
tasks.purpose: Exercise the critical workflows called out in the PRD so regressions in capture, readiness, snapshotting, close/reopen, and auto-commit are caught early.
tasks.input: Completed feature flows from workstreams B through L and PRD section 24 priority workflows.
tasks.output: Automated or scriptable end-to-end test harness covering voice invoice, receipt, snapshot, post-snapshot change, close/reopen, and auto-commit workflows.
tasks.action: Build end-to-end test scenarios with fixtures and assertions that validate the most important product journeys against domain invariants and user-visible outcomes.
verification: The harness covers all priority workflows listed in the PRD with expected events and state transitions.
Failing assertions clearly identify whether breakage is in capture, event history, readiness, snapshotting, or governance logic.
Test fixtures are deterministic and can run without live third-party services.

tasks.title: Add regression suite for export reproducibility sync safety and snapshot trust guarantees
tasks.workstream_goal: Prove that the MVP's high-risk business workflows behave predictably and safely from capture through export.
tasks.purpose: Lock down the product's core trust promises by continuously testing immutability, export reproducibility, and sync non-corruption behavior.
tasks.input: G2 export service, H2 sync worker, F1-F2 snapshot logic, and PRD sections 25, 27, and 28.
tasks.output: Regression suite focused on trust guarantees with fixtures for repeated export, changed-since-snapshot behavior, conflict replay, and history reconstruction.
tasks.action: Create regression tests that prove snapshots remain stable, repeated exports match prior outputs, sync does not corrupt committed truth, and event history can reconstruct key states.
verification: Repeated exports from the same snapshot produce matching artifacts or checksums.
Sync replay and conflict scenarios preserve local committed financial truth and emit explicit outcomes.
History reconstruction from entities plus events is sufficient to explain snapshot membership and key lifecycle changes.
